message:
fix(runtime): point Windows users to crossterm-compat regardless of native-backend feature (#66 follow-up)

The fallback `run_native` body for non-Unix-or-not-native-backend used to
prioritize the feature message: a Windows user without `native-backend`
enabled was told "enable the feature", only to find after rebuilding that
the feature gives them an Unsupported error explaining native is Unix-only.
Two-step debug.

Reorganize the cfg arms so the platform message wins: any non-Unix build
gets the "use AppBuilder::run() (crossterm-compat)" message immediately,
regardless of feature state. Unix builds without the feature still get
"enable `native-backend`". Verified compilation across all four cfg combos
(unix/non-unix × feature on/off).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
